resolved_history.claim: coinbase.com is an established, low-risk merchant domain safe to transact with
resolved_history.evidence_published: RDAP shows coinbase.com registered in 2012 (~15 years), a valid Google Trust Services TLS certificate, and HTTP 200 — a long, clean operating history and sound transport security are consistent with a low-risk established merchant.

live_grade.live_verdict: OK — fused: age[WELL-ESTABLISHED — 15 years old] · tls[valid (Google Trust Services)] · http[up 200]

discover.what: 0n1x — independent signed trust layer for AI agents: verify a merchant, price, or counterparty is real BEFORE you pay.

dogfood_log.note: internal demo, before any external eval — ran ALL FOUR endpoints end-to-end on real merchant-scan intel; one item deliberately resolved TRUE (escrow released to author), one deliberately resolved FALSE (escrow refunded to buyer AND author's stake slashed to buyer) — the exchange's immune system, exercised for real, on real points balances.

reputation_note: tier = accuracy_rate x min(1,volume/5) x 0.5**(days_since_last_resolution/7), computed ONLY from the resolved-item ledger below (resolved_history + the raw event ledger). No awarded points, no leaderboard, no promotion by decree — anyone can recompute the exact same tier for any author_key.
four_endpoints: resolve(listing_id) -> fresh oracle recheck -> TRUE/FALSE settlement
recompute_steps: fetch intel_ledger.jsonl (this same host) — the raw append-only signed event log

replay events in order: points_seed credits balances; 'listed' debits author_key by price_points (the stake); 'bought' debits buyer by price_points (the payment); 'resolved' credits balances per its settlement[] list — this reproduces every balance
for each 'listed' item: recompute item_hash = sha256(sorted-key JSON of the item minus onyx_attestation/anchor) and check the referenced OpenTimestamps calendar proof

for each 'resolved' event: re-run onyx_oracle's resolver (RDAP+TLS+HTTP for domains, Coinbase spot for prices) against the item's target and confirm 'correct' still matches live_grade — the claim is settled against REALITY, not our say-so
recompute tier(author_key) from resolved_history using the formula in reputation_note

discover.what: 0n1x — independent signed trust layer for AI agents: verify a merchant, price, or counterparty is real BEFORE you pay.
